message:
feat: workspace filtering, WCAG theme fixes, TUI UX enhancements, daemon hardening

This commit brings together several improvements across analytics,
daemon reliability, HTML export correctness, source probing safety,
and TUI usability.

## Analytics: workspace_id filtering (src/analytics/query.rs, src/ui/analytics_charts.rs)

- `build_where_parts()` now accepts an optional `workspace_column`
  parameter. When provided and the filter contains workspace IDs, a
  `workspace_id IN (...)` clause is appended to the SQL WHERE. This
  enables per-workspace filtering on Track A tables (usage_daily) while
  correctly omitting it for Track B tables (token_daily_stats) that lack
  the column.
- All four query entry points (tokens timeseries, cost timeseries,
  breakdown, tools) pass the appropriate workspace column or None.
- `resolve_workspace_filter_ids()` introduced in analytics_charts.rs to
  resolve workspace filters by both numeric ID and filesystem path,
  querying the `workspaces` table when a path string is provided.
- Existing tests updated for new signature; three new integration tests
  verify workspace filtering on breakdown, tokens timeseries, and tools.
  Two new unit tests cover the enabled/disabled workspace column cases.

## Daemon hardening (src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/resource.rs)

- Added `memory_limit_exceeded()` method that checks configured memory
  limit against actual RSS and triggers a graceful shutdown when exceeded,
  preventing unbounded memory growth in long-running daemon processes.
- `apply_nice()` and `apply_ionice()` return values are now checked; on
  failure a structured warning is logged instead of silently continuing.
- `ionice` unavailability log level upgraded from debug to warn so
  operators notice missing system tools in production.

## HTML export: character-based collapse threshold (src/html_export/renderer.rs)

- Collapse threshold comparison changed from byte length (`content.len()`)
  to character count (`content.chars().count()`), fixing incorrect
  collapsing of messages with multi-byte UTF-8 content (e.g. accented
  characters, CJK, emoji). A 60-character string of "e-acute" is 120
  bytes and was incorrectly collapsed at a threshold of 100.
- New helper `byte_index_for_char_count()` converts a character limit to
  a safe byte offset for slicing, complementing the existing
  `truncate_to_char_boundary()`.
- Both `render_message()` and `render_message_group()` updated. The
  "Click to expand (N chars)" label now shows character count instead of
  byte count.
- New test `test_collapse_threshold_uses_character_count` verifies the fix.

## Source probe safety (src/sources/probe.rs)

- The embedded shell script that counts JSONL/JSON files now applies
  `-maxdepth 8` to prevent runaway traversal of very deep directory trees.
- A 5-second timeout is applied via `timeout` (Linux) or `gtimeout`
  (macOS/Homebrew) when available, with a plain fallback when neither
  exists. This prevents the probe from hanging on network-mounted or
  extremely large session directories.

## TUI UX improvements (src/ui/app.rs)

### Query editing enhancements
- Ctrl+U now performs Unix-style line-kill (delete from start to cursor)
  instead of clearing the entire query. The old clear-all moved to Ctrl+L.
- Ctrl+K added for forward-kill (delete from cursor to end of line).
- Both operations push an undo snapshot and trigger search debounce.
- New CassMsg variants: QueryLineKilled, QueryForwardKilled.
- Ctrl+Shift+N added for history-backward navigation (complement to
  Ctrl+N for history-forward and Ctrl+R for history cycle).
- Help overlay keyboard shortcut section expanded with entries for
  Ctrl+U, Ctrl+K, Ctrl+L, Ctrl+Z, Ctrl+Shift+Z, Ctrl+R/Ctrl+N, and
  Ctrl+Shift+N.

### Detail pane toggle (Alt+D)
- New `detail_pane_hidden` flag and `DetailPaneToggled` message allow
  users to hide the detail preview pane, giving the full content width
  to the results list. Alt+D toggles the pane; when hidden, focus
  automatically returns to the results list and scroll/page events
  operate on results rather than the hidden detail area.

### Empty-state and zero-results UX overhaul
- The empty results area now distinguishes "no query submitted yet"
  (onboarding tips) from "query returned zero results" (actionable
  hints including did-you-mean suggestions, filter check prompts, and
  match mode toggle).
- Detail pane empty state now distinguishes "no results at all" (search
  prompt) from "results exist but none selected" (selection prompt).
- Hint text is centered vertically in the detail pane.
- Search input placeholder changed from "<type to search>" to the more
  descriptive "Search sessions, messages, code..." in italic style.

### Toast notification rendering
- New `render_toasts()` method draws small popups in the top-right
  corner using theme-derived colors for background, border, and
  type-specific accent (success/error/warning/info).
- Border color shifts from accent to neutral as the toast ages (>50%
  remaining fraction).
- Toasts now fired on: theme toggle, export success/failure, slow
  search (>=1s), index refresh completion/failure, and did-you-mean
  suggestion application.

### Help overlay mouse interaction
- `help_overlay_popup_area()` computes the centered 70% popup rect.
- Mouse scroll events (up/down) on the help overlay now scroll its
  content by 3 lines.
- Clicking outside the help popup dismisses it (unless pinned).
- Clicking inside the popup is a no-op (keeps help open).

### Suggestion application improvements
- `SuggestionApplied` now also applies `suggested_filters` when present,
  not just `suggested_query`, and sets cursor position to end of query.
- A toast notification confirms which suggestion was applied.

## WCAG contrast fixes (src/ui/components/theme.rs)

- Adjusted hint and fg colors across 7 theme palettes to meet WCAG AA
  or AA-large contrast ratios against their respective background/surface
  colors:
  - SolarizedDark: fg brightened to base1 (#93a1a1), hint lightened
  - SolarizedLight: fg darkened, hint darkened, user green darkened,
    system yellow darkened
  - OneDark: hint lightened
  - RosePine: hint lightened
  - Everforest: hint lightened
  - Kanagawa: hint lightened
  - AyuMirage: hint lightened
- Issue tracking entry added in .beads/issues.jsonl for traceability.

## Beads issue tracker (.beads/issues.jsonl, .beads/last-touched)

- New closed issue recorded: coding_agent_session_search-1dl2u tracking
  the WCAG contrast fix work.
- last-touched updated to reflect the new issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -106,6 +106,7 @@
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1d42ae1e","title":"Cursor-Based Pagination","description":"# Cursor-Based Pagination

## Problem Statement
Offset-based pagination (`--offset N`) has issues:
- Results can shift if index changes between pages
- Hard to track position reliably
- Inefficient for large offsets

## Proposed Solution
Add cursor-based pagination:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --limit 10
# Returns: {"next_cursor": "abc123", "hits": [...]}

cass search "error" --json --limit 10 --cursor "abc123"
# Returns next page
```

## Design Decisions

### Cursor Encoding
Cursor contains:
- Query hash (to validate same query)
- Last seen sort key (score + id)
- Expiration timestamp

Encoded as base64 for URL safety.

### Cursor Expiration
Cursors expire after 1 hour to prevent stale state issues.

### Fallback
If cursor is invalid/expired, return error with hint to restart.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--cursor` parameter for continuing pagination
- [ ] `next_cursor` in response when more results available
- [ ] Cursor validates query hasn't changed
- [ ] Expired cursors return clear error
- [ ] Works with all output formats

## Effort Estimate
Medium - 3-4 hours. Requires cursor encoding and validation logic.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.002460810Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T05:04:40.516945Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

2) rsync + SFTP fallback both exercised (force rsync unavailable scenario).

4) Artifacts stored under test-results/e2e/sources/<test>/.","notes":"Notes:

All 50 ui_snap tests now pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-21T23:04:27.069541172Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-21T23:04:30.409083169Z","closed_at":"2026-02-21T23:04:30.409064394Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","title":"T2: Mock Elimination - Replace Transitional Mocks","description":"# Epic: Eliminate Transitional Mocks

## Goal
Remove all transitional mock patterns from the codebase, replacing them with real implementations or test fixtures.

## Current Transitional Mocks (from no_mock_allowlist.json)
1. tests/connector_claude.rs - mock_claude variable
2. tests/fs_errors.rs - mock_claude variable
3. tests/pages_bundle.rs - fake_config

## Review Deadline
All transitional mocks scheduled for removal by 2026-03-26

## Approach
- Create real fixture directories from actual session data
- Use ConversationFixtureBuilder for deterministic test data
- Replace fake configs with real PagesConfig instances

## Dependencies
- Blocked by T1 (need unit tests before removing mocks)
- Referenced in downstream task bd-1dfc","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T04:19:57.403736141Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637335096Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T05:27:49.637268994Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1dlw","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3fbl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1e1s","title":"T6.0: Coverage baseline + gap report (no-mock focus)","description":"## Scope
- Generate current unit/integration coverage report (excluding tests/ & benches/)
- Identify top 20 uncovered branches/lines
- Map uncovered areas to fixture-based test additions (no mocks)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Coverage report artifact saved under test-results/coverage/
- Gap list with file:line and suggested fixture source
- Follow-up beads created for any large uncovered modules","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:46:16.901749993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:09:34.842189012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:09:34.842046477Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1e1s","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-32fs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-1f3l","title":"T6.6: Sweep remaining test mocks -> fixtures","description":"## Files
- tests/deploy_github.rs
- tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs
- tests/e2e_search_index.rs
- tests/fs_errors.rs
- tests/pages_bundle.rs
- tests/pages_pipeline_e2e.rs
- tests/search_pipeline.rs
- tests/setup_workflow.rs

## Work
- Replace mock/fake/stub patterns with fixture data or real artifacts
- Standardize fixture directories

## Acceptance Criteria
- No mock/fake/stub patterns in these tests
- validate_ci.sh --no-mock-only passes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T05:47:11.189857322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T06:22:34.181993409Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T06:22:34.181853259Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-1f3l","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-32fs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}

@@ -318,7 +318,14 @@ for dir in ~/.claude/projects ~/.codex/sessions ~/.cursor \
         SIZE=$(du -sm "$expanded_dir" 2>/dev/null | cut -f1)
         # Count JSONL files for session estimate
         if [ -d "$expanded_dir" ]; then
-            COUNT=$(find "$expanded_dir" \( -name "*.jsonl" -o -name "*.json" \) 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
+            # Keep probe bounded for very large trees: depth-limit and timeout when available.
+            if command -v timeout &> /dev/null; then
+                COUNT=$(timeout 5s find "$expanded_dir" -maxdepth 8 \( -name "*.jsonl" -o -name "*.json" \) 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
+            elif command -v gtimeout &> /dev/null; then
+                COUNT=$(gtimeout 5s find "$expanded_dir" -maxdepth 8 \( -name "*.jsonl" -o -name "*.json" \) 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
+            else
+                COUNT=$(find "$expanded_dir" -maxdepth 8 \( -name "*.jsonl" -o -name "*.json" \) 2>/dev/null | wc -l | tr -d ' ')
+            fi
         else
             COUNT=1  # Single file
         fi
